info.name: PermitoAI REST API
info.description:
REST API endpoints for PermitoAI — AI-powered permit-to-work management system for Nigerian oil & gas operations.

Base URL: {{base_url}} (default: http://localhost:4000)

**Tools** — direct wrappers for each MCP tool:
- HAZARD_SUGGEST: Identify workplace hazards using AI + historical incident data
- RISK_ASSESS: Score hazards with rule-based risk matrix (likelihood × severity) + confidence scoring
- COMPLIANCE_CHECK: Validate against DPR EGASPIN, ISO 45001, IOGP
- PERMIT_VALIDATE: Multi-layer validation (rule-based → semantic → compliance → anomaly)
- ANOMALY_DETECT: Detect copy-paste, duplicates, suspicious patterns
- SIMOPS_CHECK: Detect schedule conflicts and incompatible simultaneous work types

**Agent workflows** — orchestrated multi-step pipelines:
- full-assessment: Complete pipeline (all 4 tools chained)
- quick-assess: Fast 2-step pipeline (hazard-suggest → risk-assess)
- simops-assess: Full SIMOPS workflow (conflict check → hazard suggest → risk score → AI safety briefing)

Core principle: AI assists, rules constrain, humans decide, logs remember.

variable.description: Base URL for the PermitoAI REST API server

request.description: Returns the API server status, version, and list of available tools.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "status": "ok",
  "service": "PermitoAI REST API",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-24T10:00:00.000Z",
  "tools": ["HAZARD_SUGGEST", "RISK_ASSESS", "COMPLIANCE_CHECK", "PERMIT_VALIDATE", "ANOMALY_DETECT", "SIMOPS_CHECK"]
}

item.name: Tools
item.description: Direct wrappers for each MCP tool. Each endpoint mirrors the corresponding MCP tool exactly.

request.description:
Identifies 5-10 workplace hazards for a permit-to-work scenario using AI analysis, vector-based retrieval of DPR/IOGP regulations, and historical incident data from the Qdrant database.

**Input:** JobContext
**Returns:** Array of hazards with categories, likelihood/severity ratings, recommended controls, and DPR references.

response.name: 200 OK — Hazards identified
400 Bad Request — Invalid body

body.raw:
{
  "hazards": [
    {
      "name": "H2S Exposure",
      "category": "chemical",
      "likelihood": 3,
      "severity": 2,
      "recommendedControls": [
        "Personal H2S monitor worn at all times",
        "Wind sock installed at work site",
        "SCBA available on standby",
        "Buddy system enforced"
      ],
      "dprReference": "DPR EGASPIN Section 4.1.2 — H2S Safety Requirements",
      "explanation": "Sour gas field operations carry inherent H2S risk from reservoir gases."
    },
    {
      "name": "Fall from Height during scaffold inspection",
      "category": "physical",
      "likelihood": 2,
      "severity": 3,
      "recommendedControls": [
        "Full body harness with double lanyard",
        "Scaffold inspection before use",
        "Safety net installed below work area"
      ],
      "explanation": "Work at elevation on scaffold structures increases fall risk."
    },
    {
      "name": "Fire and explosion from welding sparks",
      "category": "physical",
      "likelihood": 2,
      "severity": 4,
      "recommendedControls": [
        "Fire blanket around hot work area",
        "Continuous gas monitoring",
        "Fire extinguisher at work site"
      ],
      "dprReference": "DPR EGASPIN Section 5.2.3",
      "explanation": "Welding sparks near hydrocarbon-containing equipment create ignition risk."
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Scores hazards against the risk matrix (likelihood × severity). Applies rule-based severity floor constraints:
- H₂S: minimum severity 4
- Explosion/asphyxiation: minimum severity 5
- Confined space / fall from height: minimum severity 4
- Hot work / dropped objects: minimum severity 3

Returns risk levels: critical (≥15), high (≥10), medium (≥5), low (<5).

New summary fields include: totalMatrixSum, averageRiskScore, dominantRiskLevel, overallAdvice, confidenceScore (0–1), and 95% confidenceInterval.

response.name: 200 OK — Risk scores
response.body:

request.description:
Validates the permit against three regulatory frameworks:
- **DPR EGASPIN** — Nigerian Department of Petroleum Resources regulations
- **ISO 45001** — Occupational Health & Safety Management Systems
- **IOGP** — International Association of Oil & Gas Producers standards

Uses Google Gemini AI as a regulatory compliance expert.

response.name: 200 OK — Compliance results

request.description:
Runs four validation layers in sequence:
1. **Layer 1 (Rule-based):** Fast checks — minimum hazards, controls present, DPR references for high-severity
2. **Layer 2 (Semantic):** AI analyzes logical consistency and completeness
3. **Layer 3 (Compliance):** Standards validation against DPR/ISO/IOGP
4. **Layer 4 (Anomaly):** Detects copy-paste, duplicates, suspicious patterns

Returns recommendation: `Recommend Approval` or `Flag for Review`.

response.name: 200 OK — Validation results

request.description:
Detects suspicious patterns in hazard assessments that suggest copy-pasting or negligence:
- Duplicate hazard names
- All identical likelihood/severity ratings (copy-paste indicator)
- All hazards rated as low risk (understatement)
- Identical control measures across all hazards

Confidence score: 0.9 (rule-based, deterministic).

response.name: 200 OK — Anomalies detected
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "anomaliesDetected": true,
  "issueCount": 3,
  "issues": [
    "All hazards have identical likelihood and severity ratings — possible copy-paste. Review individually.",
    "All hazards classified as low risk. Verify assessments are thorough and not understated.",
    "All hazards share identical control measures — controls should be hazard-specific."
  ],
  "confidence": 0.9
}

response.name: 200 OK — No anomalies
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "anomaliesDetected": false,
  "issueCount": 0,
  "issues": [],
  "confidence": 0.9
}

request.description:
Checks a new permit request for two types of SIMOPS (Simultaneous Operations) conflicts against a list of existing permits:

1. **Schedule conflicts** — same work type + overlapping work area + overlapping date range
2. **Incompatibility flags** — dangerous work type combinations regardless of area (e.g. Hot Work + Confined Space Entry, Hot Work + Gas Testing)

`workArea` in the request is optional (nullable). When omitted, only incompatibility flags are checked against all active permits.

**Incompatible pairs (examples):**
- Hot Work + Confined Space Entry → critical
- Hot Work + Gas Testing / Gas Work → critical
- Hot Work + H2S / Toxic Gas Work → critical
- Radiography / NDT + any work in same area → high
- Blasting / Explosives + any work in same area → critical

response.name: 200 OK — Conflicts found
200 OK — No conflicts

item.name: Agent
item.description: Orchestrated multi-step AI agent workflows that chain multiple tools together in a single request.

request.description: Returns metadata for all available MCP tools and agent workflows, including endpoint paths, descriptions, and expected input schemas.

body.raw:
{
  "jobType": "Confined Space Entry",

  "equipment": [
    "SCBA breathing apparatus",
    "4-gas monitor (O2, LEL, H2S, CO)",

  ],

    "name": "Pioneer Pipeline Services",
    "tier": 1
  },

}

request.description:
**Full 4-step permit assessment pipeline:**

1. **HAZARD_SUGGEST** — AI identifies hazards using RAG (regulations + historical incidents)
2. **RISK_ASSESS** — Scores hazards with rule-based safety constraints + confidence scoring
3. **COMPLIANCE_CHECK** — Validates against DPR EGASPIN, ISO 45001, IOGP *(parallel with step 4)*
4. **PERMIT_VALIDATE** — Multi-layer validation *(parallel with step 3)*

Returns all step results plus an overall recommendation.

⚠️ Takes 15-30 seconds due to multiple AI calls.

response.name: 200 OK — Full assessment result

body.raw:
{
  "jobType": "Electrical Isolation (LOTO)",
  "location": "Escravos Gas Plant, Delta State",
  "environment": "High-voltage switchgear room — 33kV distribution panel",
  "equipment": [
    "Voltage tester (non-contact)",
    "Insulated gloves (Class 4)",
    "Insulated tools",
    "Lockout/tagout kit",
    "Arc flash suit"
  ],
  "contractor": {
    "name": "Electra Engineering Nigeria",
    "tier": 2
  },
  "description": "Scheduled maintenance of 33kV switchgear panel. Full isolation required. System supplies power to gas compression train."
}

request.description:
**Fast 2-step assessment:**

1. **HAZARD_SUGGEST** — AI identifies hazards
2. **RISK_ASSESS** — Scores hazards with safety constraints + confidence scoring

If critical or high risks are detected, `requiresFullAssessment: true` is returned as a signal to run the full pipeline.

Faster than full-assessment (~8-12 seconds).

response.name: 200 OK — Quick assessment result

request.description:
**Full SIMOPS assessment pipeline (4 steps):**

1. **SIMOPS_CHECK** — Detects schedule conflicts and incompatible work type pairs
2. **HAZARD_SUGGEST** — Suggests hazards for the requested work type AND each conflicting type (in parallel)
3. **RISK_ASSESS** — Scores all combined hazards with rule-based constraints
4. **AI Safety Briefing** — Gemini generates a structured SIMOPS safety briefing based on conflicts and hazards

**Response includes:**
- `recommendation`: `HOLD` | `PROCEED WITH CONTROLS` | `SAFE TO PROCEED`
- `overallRisk`: `none` | `medium` | `high` | `critical`
- `steps.simopsCheck`: schedule conflicts + incompatibility flags
- `steps.safetyBriefing`: situationSummary, keyRisks, per-conflict mitigations, rationale

`jobContext` field is optional — use it to enrich hazard suggestions with location/environment context.

⚠️ Takes 15-25 seconds due to parallel AI calls.

response.name: 200 OK — SIMOPS conflicts found
200 OK — No conflicts

item.name: Routing (Feature 1 — Intelligent Permit Routing)
item.description:
AI-powered permit routing recommendations and pre-submission validation.

- **recommend**: Given permit data + available users, returns the optimal approver list, routing path, missing controls, risk rating, and SIMOPS conflicts.
- **pre-submission-check**: Two-layer validation (rule-based + AI semantic) that flags completeness issues before a permit is submitted.

request.description:
Returns the recommended approval routing path for a permit.

**Workflow:**
1. Computes risk rating (severity × likelihood matrix)
2. Determines required roles based on work type, isolation sections, and hazard keywords
3. Selects and load-balances available approvers by queue depth
4. AI identifies missing control measures and generates routing notes
5. Detects SIMOPS conflicts with active permits (date + area overlap)

**Returns:** recommendedApprovers, routingPath, missingControls, riskRating, simopsConflicts, confidence

response.name: 200 OK

body.raw:
{
  "permit": {
    "type": "Hot Work Permit",
    "workType": "Hot Work - Welding/Cutting",
    "workArea": "Process Areas – Module 3",
    "severity": "High",
    "likelihood": "Likely",
    "hazards": ["Fire", "Burns"],
    "controlMeasures": ["Fire extinguisher on standby"],
    "isolationSections": [],
    "startDate": "2026-04-10",
    "endDate": "2026-04-12",
    "workShift": "Day",
    "attachments": []
  }
}

request.description:
Validates permit completeness before submission.

**Layer 1 — Rule-based:** Checks required fields, isolation plan for applicable work types, attachment requirements for high-risk types, gas test control for Hot Work.

**Layer 2 — AI semantic:** Contextual review for gaps not caught by rules.

**Returns:** ready (boolean), issues array with field + message, suggestions array.

response.name: 200 OK — Not Ready

item.name: Fraud (Feature 2 — Behavioral Anomaly Detection)
item.description:
Fraud and consistency checks for permits, users, and facility-wide batch scans.

- **permit-check**: Runs self-approval, signature role mismatch, credential sharing, timestamp tampering, isolation integrity, audit trail, and duplicate checks on a single permit.
- **user-anomaly**: Computes behavioral baseline and flags statistical deviations (>2 std dev) for a specific user.
- **scan**: Batch rule-based fraud scan across multiple permits + user activity for a facility/date range.

request.description:
Runs all fraud and consistency checks on a single permit.

**Checks performed:**
- Self-approval (issuerId === approverId)
- Signature role mismatch (claimed role ≠ actual role)
- Credential sharing (same IP+UserAgent, different users, <60s apart)
- Timestamp tampering (signature before permit creation)
- Isolation self-verify (same person isolates and verifies)
- Restoration self-verify (same person restores and verifies)
- Isolation timestamp sequence (verifier before isolator)
- Missing audit trail (approved with no submit_permit entry)
- Near-duplicate detection
- AI contextual anomaly enrichment

**Input:** permit data, auditLogs, userRoles map (userId → roleName), similarPermits
**Returns:** flagged, severity, riskScore, summary, anomalies array

response.name: 200 OK — Flagged

request.description:
Behavioral anomaly report for a specific user.

**Baseline metrics computed:**
- Per-hour action frequency (mean + 2× std dev threshold)
- Off-hours activity detection (>6h from peak activity hour)
- Approval ratio anomaly (>80% of permit actions are approvals)

**Returns:** flagged, severity, riskScore, anomalies, behavioral baseline stats

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Batch fraud scan across all permits and users for a facility within a date range.

Runs rule-based checks on every permit (no per-permit AI call for performance), then performs user-level frequency analysis. AI summarises the results and provides an executive overview.

**Returns:** flaggedPermits list, flaggedUsers list, executiveSummary, topRisks, recommendedActions

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Analytics (Feature 3 — Trend & Predictive Analytics)
item.description:
Trend analysis, predictive risk modeling, incident correlation, and executive compliance reporting.

All endpoints accept a `permits` array (sourced from `GET /api/permits?page=1&limit=500` on the ePTW backend) and optional `auditLogs`.

request.description:
Computes permit trend metrics from a batch of permits over a time range.

**Computed metrics:**
- Monthly permit volume (total, approved, rejected, suspended)
- Top 10 hazard frequencies with percentages
- Monthly average risk score trend
- Top work areas by count and dominant severity
- Work type distribution
- Day vs Night shift breakdown
- Top rejection reasons
- Average approval time (from audit logs: submit_permit → approve_permit)
- AI strategic insights and operational recommendations

**Returns:** period, summary, permitVolume[], topHazards[], riskTrend[], topWorkAreas[], workTypeDistribution, shiftBreakdown, topRejectionReasons, insights

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Predicts upcoming high-risk periods, approval bottlenecks, and recommended proactive actions based on historical permit patterns.

**Computed patterns:**
- Peak submission day of week
- High-risk work areas (avg risk ≥6 or rejection rate ≥20%)
- Approval bottleneck approvers (avg >24h or >5 pending permits)
- Peak volume months

**AI generates:** highRiskPeriods for next 4 weeks, predictedBottlenecks, recommendedActions

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Identifies which combinations of work type, area, shift, and hazard count correlate with higher historical incident risk.

**Combinations analysed:**
- workType | shift
- workType | workArea
- workType | workArea | shift (triple combo)
- workArea | shift | hazard count bucket

**Minimum sample size: 3 permits per combination.**

**Returns:** correlations ranked by avg risk score, topRiskCombinations (triple), AI narrative insights and targeted mitigations

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Executive-level compliance summary across all facilities in a company.

**Computed metrics:**
- Overall approval, rejection, suspension rates
- Checklist completion rate (true/completed/done values in completionChecklist object)
- Isolation completion rate (sections with verifierId + verifierApprovedAt)
- Per-facility breakdown with compliance score
- Top suspension reasons
- AI executive summary, compliance rating (EXCELLENT/GOOD/FAIR/POOR), key risks, strategic recommendations

**Returns:** reportPeriod, overallMetrics, facilityBreakdown, topSuspensionReasons, executiveInsights

response.name: 200 OK
